trigger_evals.query: Split this roadmap into parallel Codex worktree threads and give each one a /goal.
Create separate branches and Codex threads for these phases, then track the automatically reviewed PR wave.
Run parent QA across the five child PRs and clean up the worktrees once everything is merged.
Make this one-line bug fix in the current checkout.
Delete my stale local branches in this repo.
Give me a high-level project plan but do not create threads yet.

behavior_evals.prompt: Review this repo and split the next roadmap wave into parallel Codex worktree threads.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Inspects live repo state first, proposes independently actionable slices, and creates child thread prompts with branch names, /goal text, success criteria, validation commands, and overlap risks.
checks: Inspects current branch, dirty state, existing worktrees, relevant docs, tests, and in-flight branches before slicing
Creates slices with clear deliverables and minimal overlap
Assigns each slice a branch name
Includes focused validation commands per child
Starts each child prompt with /goal
Names integration risks for shared files or surfaces
Preserves unrelated local changes

behavior_evals.prompt: Set up the Codex child threads and monitor them every 30 minutes.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Uses Codex worktree-backed project threads and app-level wave monitoring when requested, treats pending worktree setup as expected when applicable, respects publication boundaries, relies on automatic Codex Cloud PR review, and avoids shell polling.
checks: Uses Codex thread/worktree capabilities rather than normal shell-only worktrees for child execution
Seeds each child with scope, branch, success criteria, validation, and automatic-review expectations
Includes do-not-commit/push/PR instructions when publication has not been authorized
Treats pending worktree ids as expected setup state instead of failure
Uses app-level wave monitoring or heartbeat automation when requested
Uses a 30-minute parent heartbeat by default when the user asks for wave monitoring without a cadence
Uses monitors to check automatic review results and unresolved findings when child PRs are waiting on Codex Cloud
Does not create per-PR monitors merely to trigger Codex reviews
Assumes pushed PR updates trigger automatic Codex Cloud review when configured
Does not create shell sleep loops or background polling scripts
Checks child thread status, branch state, PR state, review threads, checks, and blockers
Reports missing Codex thread or automation surfaces instead of pretending children or monitors were created

behavior_evals.prompt: The child PRs are up. Be final QA for the wave before we call it done.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Performs parent-side verification across child PRs, including automatic Codex review-thread state, CI, integration conflicts, and broad validation, rather than only relaying child summaries.
checks: Fetches current remote state
Inspects all child PRs, automatic Codex review results, and review-thread state
Checks for cross-PR file overlap or merge conflicts
Runs integration merges or equivalent simulation when overlap risk is meaningful
Runs broad validation matching the wave's blast radius
Verifies automatic review on the merged head after conflict resolution when review-loop scope requires it
Does not declare the wave complete based only on child-reported status

behavior_evals.prompt: All the PRs merged. Clean up the Codex worktrees and branches.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Verifies merge state and cleanliness before removing obsolete worktrees and local branches, and leaves unrelated dirty worktrees or stashes alone.
checks: Confirms relevant PRs are merged or intentionally abandoned before cleanup
Confirms relevant worktrees are clean
Fetches and prunes remotes
Fast-forwards the primary checkout before deleting local branches
Removes only obsolete worktrees
Deletes only safe local branches
Verifies final worktree and branch inventory
Does not delete unrelated stashes or dirty linked worktrees without explicit scope
